strengths: Most intelligent Claude model for agents and coding
Excellent reasoning quality on ambiguous multi-step problems
Strong long-context synthesis for high-stakes outputs
best_for: Critical reports and decision memos
Hard architecture or strategy decisions
Complex agent planning with many constraints
Complex and production grade coding tasks
avoid_when: High-throughput simple transforms where cost is primary
Low-complexity tasks where a faster, cheaper model is sufficient

strengths: Best Claude balance of speed and intelligence
Reliable coding, editing, and tool-use workflows
Strong general-purpose instruction following
best_for: Default model for general agent work
Code generation, refactoring, and iterative debugging
Drafting and analysis tasks at production scale
avoid_when: When you need the absolute highest reasoning quality
Ultra-low-cost, ultra-high-volume batch processing

strengths: Fastest Claude model with strong quality for its speed tier
Low-cost option for high-throughput production traffic
Supports extended thinking for compact reasoning tasks
best_for: Real-time or near-real-time application paths
Large-volume routine assistant and extraction workloads
Sub-agent tasks where latency and cost dominate
avoid_when: When maximum reasoning depth is required
When tasks require adaptive thinking selection
When context needs regularly exceed its context window

strengths: Flagship GPT model for complex reasoning and coding
Strong agentic workflows with broad tool support
Reliable structured outputs and instruction adherence
best_for: Complex agent orchestration and multi-tool tasks
High-quality coding and technical problem solving
Schema-constrained or workflow-integrated responses
avoid_when: Ultra-low-cost or ultra-low-latency batch operations
Simple requests where mini or nano class models are enough

strengths: Cost-effective non-thinking mode for fast responses
Good price-performance for general coding and analysis
Supports JSON output and tool calling
best_for: Budget-sensitive coding and assistant flows
High-volume workloads prioritizing speed and cost
Routine structured tasks with moderate complexity
avoid_when: Complex reasoning tasks that benefit from explicit thinking mode
Very long, multi-step analyses needing larger reasoning traces

strengths: Thinking mode optimized for deeper step-by-step reasoning
Strong for complex coding, math, and analytical decomposition
Supports JSON output and tool calling
best_for: Complex reasoning-heavy coding and analytical tasks
Difficult problem solving where solution quality matters
Cases that benefit from longer generated reasoning outputs
avoid_when: Ultra-low-latency request paths
Simple high-volume tasks where non-thinking mode is sufficient

strengths: Latest GLM flagship base model
Strong coding capabilities and agentic long-horizon planning
Large context and output limits for complex workflows
best_for: Complex agent workflows requiring long planning and execution
Coding-intensive tasks and technical assistants
Mixed-language enterprise workflows
avoid_when: Ultra-low-latency or minimal-cost inference paths
Very simple chat tasks where lightweight models are enough

strengths: Frontier-class quality at low cost in the Flash-Lite tier
Optimized for fast, high-volume multimodal workloads
Strong price-performance for routine reasoning tasks
best_for: Latency-sensitive and budget-sensitive production workloads
Large-scale request volume with acceptable quality
General multimodal assistant tasks
avoid_when: Deepest reasoning and hardest problem solving
Highest-stakes technical outputs where Pro is preferred

strengths: Frontier-class performance at a fraction of larger-model cost
Strong multimodal understanding with fast response times
Good quality-speed tradeoff for production assistants
best_for: General-purpose multimodal agent and assistant workloads
Balanced coding, analysis, and content generation
Applications needing strong quality with lower latency than Pro
avoid_when: Most complex reasoning tasks best handled by Pro
Extreme cost-minimization scenarios better suited to Lite

strengths: Advanced intelligence for complex problem solving
Strong deep reasoning, coding, and agentic capabilities
High-quality synthesis for challenging multi-step tasks
best_for: Hard reasoning and complex technical workflows
Advanced coding and high-quality analytical synthesis
High-stakes tasks where answer quality is prioritized
avoid_when: Latency-critical high-volume paths
Routine low-complexity tasks where Flash or Flash-Lite is enough

low.use_when: Simple extraction, formatting, or direct Q&A.

low-medium.use_when: Routine business logic with small ambiguity.

medium.use_when: Most coding, analysis, and report drafting tasks.

medium-high.use_when: Non-trivial planning and higher quality synthesis.

adaptive.use_when: When you want the agent to choose based on task tags and budget sensitivity. This only works for Claude Opus and Sonnet.
adaptive.target: let the agent pick the best model for each task
